mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer discretionary and durable goods demand, driving corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in quarterly return regressions or positive return frequency falls below 50% when both series trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and higher capacity utilization, which compresses input costs relative to output prices and boosts corporate profit margins, yielding positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during mid-cycle expansion or Rank IC turns negative over any 24-month rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of threshold-crossing instances or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline or positive return frequency drops below 52% during concurrent labor improvements.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate revenue and earnings trajectories, providing fundamental support for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with forward equity returns (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals severe supply-side disruption or demand collapse, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the threshold condition fails to generate a significant return differential (t-test p > 0.05).
